title: Dental pain/toothache (starter evidence summary)
text:
Dental pain can be caused by cavities, cracked teeth, gum infection, or abscess.
Telephone triage focuses on airway risk and spreading infection.

Emergency red flags (ER now):
- Trouble breathing, drooling, inability to swallow, or rapidly progressing swelling

Urgent evaluation (same-day):
- Facial/jaw swelling, fever, feeling very ill (possible abscess/spreading infection)
- Immunocompromised patients with dental infection symptoms
- Severe uncontrolled pain

Supportive measures:
- OTC pain relievers (acetaminophen/ibuprofen) if safe; avoid dosing.
- Dental evaluation is often required; advise seeking in-person dental/urgent care.
